name: Advance America
description_short: Advance America El Cajon, California — Advance America is one of the largest payday lenders in the US, offering payday loans, installment loans, and tit...
description_long:
Founded in 1997 and headquartered in Spartanburg, South Carolina, Advance America is one of the largest payday lending chains in the United States by store count. The company operates as a for-profit, state-licensed consumer lender — not a nonprofit, credit union, or CDFI — and is currently a subsidiary of Purpose Financial, which itself is owned by Grupo Elektra, a Mexican retail and financial conglomerate that acquired Advance America in 2012 for approximately $780 million. Advance America holds state lending licenses in every state where it operates (roughly 23–28 states) and has earned Great Place to Work® certification for its internal culture. It is BBB-accredited (since 2024) with an A+ rating.

Advance America's core product is the payday loan — short-term cash advances typically ranging from $100 to $2,000 with repayment terms of 7 to 30 days, tied to the borrower's next paycheck. In select states, the company also offers installment loans with 3–12 month repayment periods, vehicle title loans secured against the borrower's car, and revolving lines of credit up to approximately $4,000. APRs across these products range from roughly 143% to 688%, depending on state regulations and loan type. Applications are accepted both in-store at 800–1,500+ locations nationwide and online at advanceamerica.net, with no minimum credit score stated — bad credit applicants are explicitly welcomed. In-store approvals can result in cash in hand within approximately 30 minutes; online approvals typically fund the next business day.

What differentiates Advance America from smaller payday lenders is its sheer scale and operational consistency. With a national brick-and-mortar footprint across roughly two dozen states, it offers something many fintech-only lenders cannot: in-person service for borrowers who lack reliable banking infrastructure or prefer face-to-face transactions. The company also runs a customer loyalty rewards program that allows repeat borrowers to earn points for on-time payments, redeemable as fee discounts — a rare feature in the payday lending space. Its A+ BBB accreditation and declining complaint trend (302 complaints over 3 years, with only 74 in the most recent 12 months) suggest improving customer service operations relative to its loan volume.

The most important caveat for any consumer considering Advance America is cost. APRs in the 143%–688% range make these among the most expensive legal credit products available in the United States. The BBB customer review rating of 1.7 out of 5 — driven primarily by complaints about fees and collections — reflects genuine dissatisfaction among borrowers who struggle to repay. Advance America is best used as a true last resort for a genuine short-term cash emergency, not as a routine financial tool. Consumers who can access a credit union PAL loan, employer advance, or even a credit card cash advance should exhaust those options first due to dramatically lower costs.

pricing.guarantee_details: No money-back guarantee found in public documentation. Advance America is a lender — if a loan is not drawn down, fees may not apply, but no advertised refund policy exists.

pros: One of the largest payday lenders in the US with 800–1,500+ physical store locations across ~23–28 states
In-store approvals can result in cash in hand within approximately 30 minutes
Online applications available at advanceamerica.net with next-business-day funding
Bad credit accepted — no stated minimum credit score requirement
A+ BBB rating with accreditation since 2024 and a declining complaint trend
Customer loyalty rewards program lets repeat borrowers earn points redeemable for fee discounts
Owned by Grupo Elektra, a large established financial conglomerate — not a fly-by-night operation
cons: APRs range from approximately 143% to 688% — among the highest legal borrowing costs available in the US
BBB customer review rating is only 1.7 out of 5 based on 154 reviews, primarily citing fees and collections practices
302 BBB complaints filed in the past 3 years, though the trend is declining
Not available in all US states — product availability varies significantly by state law
Title loans and lines of credit are only available in select states, limiting product access for many borrowers
best_for: Underbanked consumers with poor or no credit history who have been declined by banks and credit unions
People facing a genuine short-term cash emergency who need funds the same day in person
Borrowers who prefer in-store, face-to-face loan transactions over app-based fintech lenders
Repeat borrowers who can benefit from the loyalty rewards program to offset fees over time
diagnosis: Advance America is genuinely best for consumers in a true short-term cash emergency who have no access to cheaper credit and need funds fast — particularly those who prefer in-person service at one of its 1,000+ locations. The central caveat is cost: APRs of 143%–688% make these loans extremely expensive, and the 1.7/5 BBB customer rating signals that many borrowers find the repayment burden difficult; anyone who can qualify for a credit union PAL, personal loan, or credit card advance should use those options instead.
services: Installment Loans (3–12 month repayment, select states)
Title Loans (secured by vehicle, select states)
Lines of Credit (up to ~$4,000 revolving, select states)
In-Store Cash Advances (same-day, ~30 minutes after approval)
Customer Loyalty Rewards Program (points for on-time payments, redeemable for fee discounts)
Money Order Services (per Google business listing)

cfpb_data.cfpb_company_name: Advance America, Cash Advance Centers, Inc.
